misconceptionSummary: Students add all sides individually rather than using the formula n × side length.

visuals.ruleText: A regular polygon has all sides equal and all angles equal
visuals.subText: Perimeter = number of sides × side length

steps.narration: A regular polygon has every side the same length. That means you do not need to add each side individually — multiply the side length by the number of sides.
A regular hexagon has six equal sides. Six multiplied by four point five is twenty-seven centimetres. One multiplication replaces six additions.
If the perimeter is given, find the side length by dividing by the number of sides. Thirty-five divided by five gives seven centimetres per side.

visuals.label: Regular polygon → multiply one side length by number of sides

steps.narration: Perimeter equals n times the side length. Know the side, multiply. Know the perimeter, divide. The formula works in both directions.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: adding 4.5 + 4.5 + 4.5 + 4.5 + 4.5 + 4.5 individually. All sides are equal in a regular polygon — multiply the side length by the number of sides.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Regular means all sides equal. One multiplication gives the perimeter — n times the side length. No need to add every side separately.
